body.title: Cache palace rule-content by hash across sessions; context-load reads hash-only on hit
body.result: Added hash-gated steps 2a+2b to context-load — md5 of rule content and palace tree, writes ~/.TheIgors/claudecode/rules_hash.txt + palace_tree_hash.txt, skips full reload when unchanged (~1500 tokens saved/session)
body.description:
Optimize /context-load step 2 to cache rule and tree hashes. Compute md5(content) per rule path, composite hash over all. Write to ~/.TheIgors/claudecode/rules_hash.txt after successful load. On subsequent loads: compute current hash, compare. If unchanged: print 'rules unchanged since last session (hash=abc123)' — actual content stays resident in CC context from recent training. If changed: full load, report which specific rules changed via per-path hash diff. Step 2b (tree listing) same treatment. Saves ~1500 tokens per unchanged session (most sessions). Scope: token optimization, no behaviour change. Files: context-load/SKILL.md, new state file.

test_plan: (1) First context-load writes hash file. (2) Second context-load reads 'unchanged' without printing rules. (3) Modify a palace rule — third context-load triggers full reload and identifies changed rule by path.
